operator: HipKittens SharedKV backward latent-dKV epilogue fork
goal: Test whether the resident compressed-KV tile owner can be split from the current four-warp CTA into one warp per CTA so route masking can skip the producer body for inactive compressed tiles.

source_change.change: Add compile-time ATTN_NUM_WARPS and matching probe --num-warps so route_blocks/query_tile_mask shapes can match the static kernel grid.

primary_errors: subtile_inplace<256, 32>(K_j_smem, ...) requires shared tile rows divisible by 256, but NUM_WARPS=1 makes K_j_smem rows 64
dP_ij_bf16_col_T is an art<bf16, 256, 16, ...> tile, but attn_i_smem becomes st<bf16, 64, 16, ...>; static assertion requires matching rows

read: The existing HipKittens owner is structurally a 256-row resident KV tile schedule. Making inactive compressed tiles independently skippable is still a good direction, but it requires retile of K_j_smem, dP transpose storage/load, and associated dQ/dK/dV register tile shapes. It is not a Makefile-only change.
next_gate: Design a real 64-row resident tile owner or a route-keyed score/prob producer instead of trying to mechanically change NUM_WARPS.
